courseleaf.title: Linguistics, Master of Arts
courseleaf.informationtext:
Director of Graduate Studies: Contact the Department of Linguistics; Faculty

Graduate Study

The Department of Linguistics offers a program of study leading to M.A. and Ph.D. degrees.

The M.A. program follows PLAN II. 36-38 units of coursework are required, at least 18 of which must be graduate level courses in the major field. A comprehensive final examination in the major subject is required of each candidate. No thesis is required.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Linguistics.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Linguistics at UC Davis distinguishes itself  by offering an area of emphasis in second language acquisition and development, which responds to a growing need for researchers trained to investigate multilingualism and the acquisition and teaching of nonnative languages. Other areas of emphasis include; language and society, language and mind/brain, and language structure and theory.
